response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack/web engineer with broad polyglot experience, but the role requires deep embedded firmware, C-on-UNIX, Linux kernel modification, and network-security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching) — none of which appear in the resume. Critically, the role requires native-level Japanese, which the candidate does not list. Despite some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments a decade ago, the profile is misaligned with embedded LAN-switch security development.
strengths: Long, diverse engineering track record across systems, web, and embedded-adjacent work
Some historical C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a developer-tools product
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing, and open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — hard requirement is native-level
No evidence of Linux kernel modification, MACsec, 802.1X, or switch/embedded firmware experience
Primary recent stack is TypeScript/Node/web — far from C-based embedded networking work

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking / switch security experience
Limited recent C and Linux kernel work
